title: Can Podman command be used in WSL2 in the same way as Docker?
body:
# Docker Desktop
1.The WSL2 Windows subsystem includes an Ubuntu
2.Install Docker Desktop

The Docker command is available either in Powershell or in the WSL2 subsystem Ubuntu or other WSL2 subsystems.

In PowerShell
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6773121/191396386-4588e5d8-d4d2-4f38-bd51-5714c448e542.png)
In Ubuntu for WSL2
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6773121/191397431-2d9479de-4793-4673-94b3-57d223491fd3.png)
In Debian for WSL2
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6773121/191397443-4519c569-1fd7-4875-9825-1b25960c299c.png)

Podman can only be used in PowerShell. Is it supported?
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6773121/191397548-3d422d3f-2f22-47f6-aff1-589be97b2c8c.png)

comments.body: Is there a wsl instance called `podman-machine-default` when you run `wsl -l -v`?
